data.title: Restaurant Brands buys partners' equity stakes in Burger King China for ~$158M cash
data.description:
Restaurant Brands International (NYSE:QSR) subsidiary has acquired from TFI Asia Holdings BV and Pangaea Two Acquisition Holdings XXIII all their equity interests in Burger King China for approximately $158M in an all-cash transaction.

As a result, Restaurant Brands now owns nearly 100% of the business and will engage advisors to assist the company to identify a new local partner to inject primary capital into the business and become the controlling shareholder.

This transaction aligns with the company’s long-term strategy of partnering with experienced local operators while maintaining a primarily franchised business.

"We are thankful for TFI's and Cartesian's partnership over the years and their role in expanding the brand in China," said Rafael Odorizzi, President of Asia Pacific for RBI. "This transaction marks the beginning of a new chapter for Burger King in China and reinforces our commitment to long-term growth in the region as we identify a new local operating partner. We are committed to offering our guests high quality food and exceptional experiences in welcoming restaurants across China."

QSR +0.66% premarket to $64.95

Source: Press Release

data.title: GeneDx Holdings reports Q4 results
data.description:

data.title: Tri Pointe Homes GAAP EPS of $1.37 beats by $0.08, revenue of $1.23B beats by $10M
data.description:
• Tri Pointe Homes press release (NYSE:TPH): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.37 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $1.23B (-0.8% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Home sales revenue for the quarter was $1.2 billion, a decrease of 2%
    • New home deliveries of 1,748 homes compared to 1,813 homes, a decrease of 4%
    • Average sales price of homes delivered of $699,000 compared to $685,000, an increase of 2%
  • Net new home orders of 940 compared to 1,078, a decrease of 13%
  • Active selling communities averaged 146.8 compared to 159.3, a decrease of 8%
  • Backlog units at quarter end of 1,517 homes compared to 2,320, a decrease of 35%
  • Announced a new stock repurchase program authorizing the repurchase of up to $250 million of common stock through December 31, 2025
  • For the first quarter of 2025, the Company anticipates delivering between 900 and 1,100 homes at an average sales price between $685,000 and $695,000. The Company expects its homebuilding gross margin percentage to be in the range of 22.0% to 23.0% for the first quarter of 2025 and anticipates its SG&A expense as a percentage of home sales revenue will be in the range of 15.0% to 16.0%. Lastly, the Company expects its effective tax rate for the first quarter of 2025 to be approximately 26.0%.
  • For the full year of 2025, the Company anticipates delivering between 5,500 and 6,100 homes at an average sales price between $660,000 and $670,000. The Company expects its homebuilding gross margin percentage to be in the range of 20.5% to 22.0% for the full year of 2025 and anticipates its SG&A expense as a percentage of home sales revenue will be in the range of 11.0% to 12.0%. Lastly, the Company expects its effective tax rate for the year to be approximately 26.0%.

data.title: Chemours Non-GAAP EPS of $0.11 misses by $0.05, revenue of $1.36B misses by $10M
data.description:

data.title: Elon Musk’s xAI releases Grok 3, claims superior performance over rivals
data.description:
Grok 3, the latest iteration of the artificial intelligence chatbot developed by Elon Musk's company xAI, was released late on Monday.

In a live-streamed presentation on Monday, Musk said, “We’re very excited to present Grok-3, which is, we think, an order of magnitude more capable than Grok-2 in a very short period of time. “[It’s a] maximally truth-seeking AI, even if that truth is sometimes at odds with what is politically correct.”

Grok 3 is a family of models, including a smaller version Grok 3 mini, Grok 3 Reasoning and Grok 3 mini Reasoning. xAI has been using an enormous data center in Memphis, featuring around 200,000 GPUs, to train Grok 3.

In a post on X, Musk claimed that Grok 3 was developed with “10x” more computing power than its predecessor, Grok 2, using an expanded training data set that ostensibly includes filings from court cases.

The chatbot will be competing with several major AI chatbots, including ChatGPT (OpenAI), Gemini (Google DeepMind), Claude (Anthropic) and DeepSeek-V3.

Musk founded xAI as a challenger to Alphabet's Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) and Microsoft-backed (MSFT) OpenAI. xAI claims Grok 3 beats GPT-4o, latest AI model developed by OpenAI, on benchmarks, including GPQA (a benchmark designed to test an AI model’s ability to answer complex, graduate-level questions) and AIME (used to evaluate AI models on a sampling of math questions).

Grok 3 Reasoning and Grok 3 mini Reasoning, two models in the new Grok 3 family, can carefully “think through” problems, similar to “reasoning” models like DeepSeek’s R1 and OpenAI’s o3-mini. xAI also claims that Grok 3 Reasoning outperforms the best version of o3-mini, o3-mini-high, on several popular benchmarks, including a newer mathematics benchmark called AIME 2025.

Subscribers to X’s Premium+ tier ($50 per month) will be the first to access Grok 3, while additional features will be exclusive to a new plan introduced by xAI, called SuperGrok. If the leaks are accurate, SuperGrok will be available at $30 per month or $300 per year and offers enhanced reasoning, DeepSearch queries, and unlimited image generation.

data.title: Delta Air Lines trades lower after an incident that saw a plane flip over landing in Toronto
data.description:
Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) issued an updated statement regarding the crash landing of one of its regional jets at the Toronto Pearson International Airport on Monday.

The airline company said its incident response team deployed to Toronto Pearson International Airport Monday evening to support efforts surrounding Delta Connection flight 4819, operated by Endeavor Air, that was involved in a single-aircraft accident at YYZ around 2:15 p.m. ET.

The response team includes specially trained Delta Care Team representatives who will provide support for customers and their loved ones. Members of Endeavor Air's leadership team, including CEO Jim Graham, are also en route to ensure full cooperation with investigators.

No fatalities have been reported from the incident, and some of the customers initially transported to area hospitals have been released.

Shares of Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) were down 2.0% in premarket trading on Tuesday.

data.title: Nasdaq futures lead S&P, Dow to start shortened week
data.description:
Stock index futures edged higher Tuesday with attention on geopolitics.

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.3% and S&P futures (SPX) +0.2% were up slightly. Dow futures (INDU) were flat.

There was more action in the bond market as global rates rose. European bonds were the driver, with prices falling on speculation that the market would see new issuance as countries spent more on defense.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.52% and the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.28%.

The U.S. and Russia peace talks over Ukraine are set to begin today in Saudi Arabia. But Ukraine and European nations will not be present.

Deutsche Bank strategist Jim Reid said "potential implications are huge after the events of the last five days where first the US froze out Europe in announcing the start of talks with Russia, and second with the broadside against Europe from US Vice President JD Vance."

"That all this is occurring within a week of the German general election (this Sunday) means that Europe might start to move down a very different path within weeks to the pre-existing one."

On the macro front, the February Empire State Manufacturing Index arrives before the bell. The forecast is for a rise to -1.9, still in contraction territory.

"The initial reaction (Monday) was to price in more European defense spending with bonds selling off and equities rising, especially in the defense sector," Reid said.

data.title: Honda open to reviving merger talks if Nissan CEO exits - report
data.description:
Honda (NYSE:HMC) is ready to revive merger talks with Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY) if the latter's CEO Makoto Uchida resigns, the Financial Times reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Merger talks between the Japanese automakers fell through after Honda (NYSE:HMC) proposed that Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY) become a fully owned subsidiary, instead of the two firms being on equal footing. Honda made the pitch as it was dissatisfied with the pace of Nissan's turnaround efforts.

Honda (HMC) is prepared to restart negotiations with a new leader at Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY) who can better manage internal opposition. Uchida intends to stay at the helm till 2026, but Nissan's board is said to have begun informal discussions over when he should step down.

Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY) needs to finalize a deal soon to avert a looming cash crunch as sales continue to deteriorate. Foxconn (OTCPK:HNHAF) last week confirmed that it'd be open to acquiring Nissan shares as the world's largest contract manufacturer eyes diversifying into the electric vehicle sector. Foxconn has already approached Renault (OTCPK:RNSDF), which is looking to sell part of its 36% stake in Nissan.

However, the Japanese establishment seeks to ward off Foxconn (OTCPK:HNHAF) given its perceived closeness to China. Private equity firms like KKR (KKR) and U.S. tech companies have reportedly been asked to consider investing in Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY).
